{"problem_standardized_math":"Let \\(\\bar{x} \\in \\mathbb{R}^n\\) be given, and let \\(x^*\\) be the solution of the projection problem\n\\[\n\\min \\|x-\\bar{x}\\|^2 \\qquad \\text{subject to} \\qquad l \\le x \\le u.\n\\]\nAssume for simplicity that \\(-\\infty < l_i < u_i < \\infty\\) for all \\(i=1,2,\\ldots,n\\). Show that the solution of this problem coincides with the projection formula given by (16.69); that is, show that \\(x^* = P(\\bar{x},l,u)\\). (Hint: The problem is separable.)"}
